name: Evelyn Knight
abstract: Evelyn Knight (born Evelyn Davis, December 31, 1917 – September 28, 2007) was an American singer of the 1940s and 1950s. Damon Runyon, in one of his newspaper columns, described Knight as "a lissome blonde lassie with a gentle little voice and a face mother would not mind having brought home to her."

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Coleman
about_quotes.evidence: Evelyn wanted us to accompany her... Evelyn skipped a measure in one of the songs and the cats accompanying her didn't know how to cover up for her.

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Rowles
about_quotes.evidence: One of the finest, one of the best singers I ever played for wasn't a Jazz singer. That's the funny part of it. Evelyn Knight.
